summary: Iter 38 — Ticket PDF/JPEG/PNG attachment backend tests. Ran 20 pytest cases against the live preview URL covering POST /api/tickets (happy path with mixed pdf/jpeg/png, data-URL prefix stripping, no-attachments allowed), validation failures (>5 files → 400, >5MB decoded → 400, non-base64 → 400 'is not valid base64', mime not in allow-list → 422 Pydantic Literal), DB persistence (base64 body actually stored on the ticket doc and re-decodes to %PDF), GET /api/tickets (both scope=mine and scope=all — metadata only, no base64 leak, company_admin scope limited to their company, super_admin sees all), legacy ticket without `attachments` field returned as `attachments: []`, GET /api/tickets/{id}/attachments/{index} authZ matrix (owner-employee 200, other-employee-same-company 403, company_admin-in-company 200, company_admin-other-company 403, super_admin 200, unknown ticket_id 404, out-of-range index 404), and PATCH /api/tickets/{id} status regression. All 20 tests passed. IT38_* seed data cleaned up in fixture teardown; real super_admin (sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com) PIN fields were never touched — tests use their own seeded super_admin user + session token.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1637-1638 has dead unreachable code (a duplicate `user = ...; return {...}` block right after the real `return` inside otp_verify). Not related to this feature but worth cleaning up.
server.py is now 5032 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting routes (tickets/attendance/payroll/etc.) into their own modules.

success_rate.frontend: not tested — request was backend only

seed_data_creation: Seeded (and torn down at test end) 2 companies (IT38_A/IT38_B), 3 employees (EMPA1/EMPA2/EMPB1), 2 company_admins (ADMA/ADMB), 1 dedicated super_admin user, plus one legacy ticket without an `attachments` key for the empty-attachments-on-old-doc test. All rows tagged with IT38_ prefix and removed via pymongo teardown; verified counts == 0 post-run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Ticket attachment endpoints are fully covered by /app/backend/tests/test_iter38_ticket_attachments.py. Reuse the _mk_user pattern (direct pymongo user + user_sessions insert) to seed role-specific tokens without touching the real super_admin PIN. The `_tiny_pdf_b64`, `_tiny_jpeg_b64` helpers and the 5MB+1 zero-byte payload trick are useful for future ticket-attachment regressions.
rca_of_issue: N/A — feature working as specified across all 20 scenarios.
